list.help: List publisher accounts the authenticated user has access to.
list.usage_instructions: List publisher accounts tied to the saved token. Use `--table/-t` for readable output, `--limit/-l` to cap rows, `--filter/-f` for field filters, and `--properties/-p` to keep only selected fields.

get.help: Get one publisher account (filtered from the /accounts list).
get.usage_instructions: Fetch one publisher account by `PUBLISHER_ID`. Use this after `publishers list` when you need a single record, and add `--table/-t` for formatted output.

publishers.usage_instructions: Publisher-account lookups for the authenticated Awin user. Use this group to enumerate accessible publisher ids before running programme queries.

list.help: List advertiser programmes for the current publisher.
list.usage_instructions: List advertiser programmes for the current publisher. Use `--relationship/-r` to switch between `joined`, `pending`, and `notjoined`; use `--publisher-id` to override the saved publisher id; and use `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, and `--properties/-p` for result shaping.

get.help: Get one advertiser programme by id.
get.usage_instructions: Fetch one advertiser programme by `PROGRAMME_ID`. Use `--publisher-id` when you need a non-default publisher context, and add `--table/-t` for formatted output.

programmes.usage_instructions: Advertiser programme discovery for a publisher account. Use this group to list joined, pending, or unjoined programmes and inspect one programme by id.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Save or refresh Awin credentials. Use `--profile/-p` for a named profile, `--force/-F` to replace stored credentials, and `--credential-type/-c` only when you need to target one credential type explicitly.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored Awin credentials. Use `--profile/-p` to remove a specific profile instead of the default profile.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Check which Awin profiles have saved credentials and whether they authenticate successfully. Use `--profile/-p` for one profile and `--table/-t` for formatted output.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run an authentication smoke test against saved credentials. Use `--profile/-p` to limit the check, `--table/-t` for formatted output, and `--verbose/-v` to show detailed checks.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List saved authentication profiles. Use `--table/-t` for formatted output, `--limit/-l` to cap rows, `--filter/-f` to filter by fields, and `--properties/-p` to show only selected columns.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Show one saved auth profile by `NAME`. Use this when you need the stored/default state for a specific profile, and add `--table/-t` for formatted output.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new named auth profile from `NAME`. Run this before `auth login --profile NAME` when you want to separate multiple Awin accounts.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Set `NAME` as the default auth profile. Use this when future `awin` commands should resolve credentials from a different saved profile without passing `--profile`.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a saved auth profile by `NAME`. Use `--force` to skip the confirmation prompt.

profiles.usage_instructions: Named-profile management for Awin credentials. Use this subgroup to list profiles, inspect one profile, create a new one, change the default, or delete an old profile.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication and profile management for Awin bearer-token access. Use `auth login` to save credentials, `auth status` or `auth test` to verify them, and `auth profiles ...` to manage named profiles.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Remove cached responses for the Awin CLI. Use this when you need the next command to hit the live API instead of cached data.

cache.usage_instructions: Response-cache maintenance. Use this group only when cached API responses need to be cleared before a fresh run.

usage_instructions: Use for Awin publisher-account lookups, advertiser programme discovery, auth profile management, and cache maintenance. Commands follow `awin <group> <action>`. Use `publishers list`, `programmes list`, and `auth profiles list` when you need table/filter/limit/property controls.
